zone_6b_notes: Hardy perennial in Zone 6b â€” survives winters in Farmington with light mulching. Plant in well-drained soil; thyme rots in wet Missouri winters if drainage is poor. Woody stems die back in winter but regrow from base in spring (late March/April). Harvest before flowering for best flavor.
